source.title: Corporate Controls and Workflows — Policies Reference
source.author: Ava Chen
source.body:
Overview

Purpose: This handbook centralizes company-wide operational controls and step-by-step workflows for access & permissions, change management, data handling, procurement & expenses, vendor management, travel, and the canonical internal templates. Its goal is to reduce ambiguity, accelerate approvals, and ensure consistent compliance with our internal risk posture and partner requirements.

Audience: All Redwood employees, contractors, and teams owning operational changes or supplier relationships.

Scope: Applies to hosted, dedicated, and private deployment controls, all production and non-production environments, and all vendors engaged on Redwood contracts.

Owners and escalation contacts (quick reference)
| Area | Primary Owner | Secondary Owner | Escalation | SLA for Triage |
|---|---:|---:|---|---:|
| Access & Permissions | Security (rbac@redwood.ai) | IT Ops | Head of Security | 24 hours |
| Change Management | Eng Platform | Eng SRE | CTO | 48 hours |
| Data Management | Privacy & Legal | Eng Platform | Chief Legal Officer | 72 hours |
| Procurement & Expenses | Finance | Procurement Manager | CFO | 48 hours |
| Vendor Management | Finance-Legal | Security | Head of Partnerships | 5 business days |
| Travel & Expenses | People Ops | Finance | Head of People Ops | 3 business days |

1) Access & Permissions
Principles
- Least privilege by default.
- Role-based assignments with time-limited elevated access.
- All privileged grants require a business justification and an owner.
- Access review cadence: quarterly for production environments; semi-annually for non-production.

RBAC tiers (standardized roles)
- viewer: Read-only to logs, dashboards, and metrics.
- developer: Code and non-prod infra access; no production secrets.
- operator: Production deploy/run privileges without secret-management write.
- admin: Full control; requires 2-person approval and annual attestation.

Request and approval flow (step-by-step)
1. Submit an Access Request using the "Access Request" template (see Templates section). Include scope (systems, duration), justification, and approver (service owner).
2. Automated checks: system enforces MFA, corporate device, and current employment status.
3. Approver reviews within 48 hours.
4. If approved, Access is provisioned with a maximum duration of 30 days for elevated roles; auto-expire.
5. Requester or approver must document business rationale in the request record.

Emergency access
- For urgent operational needs, request a Break Glass session via the on-call runbook channel. A second approver must confirm the session within 8 hours. All Break Glass sessions are logged and subject to post-event review.

Periodic access audit
- Owners must attest to role membership quarterly. Audit artifacts retained for 2 years.

2) Change Management
Change classes
- Minor (low-risk): config tweaks, non-production deploys, documentation updates. No RFC required; notify owner and schedule.
- Standard (medium-risk): production config changes, model toggles, infra scaling. Requires RFC and scheduled maintenance window.
- Major (high-risk): architecture changes, schema migrations, database sharding, or anything requiring rollback coordination. Requires formal RFC, runbook, canary strategy, and SRE sign-off.

RFC template (must include)
- Title, author, date
- Change class and business justification
- Impact matrix (services, customers, data scope)
- Rollout plan (canary %, observability signals, metrics to monitor)
- Backout/rollback criteria and steps
- Validation plan and post-deploy verification checklist
- Owner and approvers

Approval cadence and SLAs
- Standard changes: approvals requested at least 48 hours in advance; approver response SLA 48 hours.
- Major changes: RFC posted and reviewed in the weekly architecture forum; approvals documented before change window.

Deployment windows and blackout periods
- Regular maintenance windows: Saturdays 02:00–06:00 UTC for non-critical infra tasks.
- Blackout: Major product launches or peak traffic periods (documented per release). No non-emergency changes during blackout.

Emergency change process
1. Triage by on-call SRE and owner.
2. Notify stakeholders via the incident channel.
3. Implement hotfix with at least one peer review and record the change per post-mortem.

3) Data Management
Classification and handling
- PUBLIC: Safe to publish.
- INTERNAL: Company information that should not be public.
- CONFIDENTIAL: Customer PII, credentials, financials. Access limited.
- SENSITIVE: Regulated data (HIPAA, GDPR PII where applicable). Additional contractual controls required.

Retention schedule (canonical)
- Application logs (non-PII): 90 days (default).
- Audit logs / access logs: 1 year (security-critical).
- Backups: 90 days snapshot, longer per retention policy for legal holds.
- Customer data: retention per customer contract; default 2 years unless specified.

Encryption and key management
- All CONFIDENTIAL and SENSITIVE data must be encrypted at rest and in transit.
- Keys must be stored in a company-managed KMS; BYOK exceptions require Legal approval.

Data deletion and subject requests
- Data deletion requests initiated via Privacy intake form. Owner: Privacy & Legal. Service owners must honor deletion within 30 days or document exception and notify Privacy.

Data access for third parties
- Only allowed after a Data Processing Agreement and Security Questionnaire are validated. Technical controls (tokenized/limited views) preferred over bulk exports.

4) Procurement & Expenses
Approval thresholds and process
- <= $5,000: Manager-level approval and corporate card use.
- $5,001–$50,000: Department head + Procurement notification; must use standard PO template.
- > $50,000: Formal Procurement RFx and Legal review; Procurement lead coordinates vendor evaluation.

Vendor selection rubric (scorecard highlights)
- Security posture (SOC2 / ISO27001): 35%
- Product fit and roadmap: 25%
- Total cost of ownership: 15%
- Reference customers and support: 15%
- Diversity and supplier risk: 10%

Contracting and payment
- Legal must review all vendor agreements prior to signature.
- Finance issues POs for contract amounts > $5k unless recurring SaaS (then follow SaaS procurement flow).
- Payment terms target: Net 30 for standard vendors; exceptions documented in the contract.

Expense policy highlights
- Receipts required for any single expense > $25.
- Per diems: domestic $75/day, international country-by-country (see Travel Appendix).
- Card misuse escalations: repeated violations may result in revocation of corporate card privileges.

5) Vendor Management
Onboarding checklist (must be completed before production work)
1. Complete Vendor Intake form and submit to Procurement.
2. Security Questionnaire AND evidence (SOC2 report or equivalent).
3. Legal reviews and signs contract template (include data handling clauses as needed).
4. Finance sets up vendor record and payment terms.
5. Technical onboarding: least-privilege credentials, IP allowlist, and expirations.
6. Kickoff meeting with success plan and QBR cadence.

Ongoing oversight
- Annual risk review for vendors with access to CONFIDENTIAL or SENSITIVE data.
- Quarterly QBRs for strategic vendors, including performance KPIs and incident review.
- Termination checklist: revoke credentials, collect data, confirm certificate of destruction when relevant.

6) Travel & Expenses
Pre-approval and booking
- All international travel requires pre-approval from People Ops and manager.
- Preferred booking channels: company travel portal. Use corporate rates where available.

Safety and compliance
- Employees traveling to high-risk countries must complete a travel risk assessment and receive security clearance before booking.
- Travel insurance and emergency contacts are provided through People Ops.

Expense submission rules
- Submit expenses within 15 days of return. Late submissions may be denied unless exception granted.
- Mandatory receipts for airfare, lodging, and meals above per-diem.

Per diem and allowable items
- Meals: up to domestic per diem. Alcohol allowed only when hosted by vendor and must be itemized.
- Ground transportation: ride-share and taxis acceptable; car rental requires justification for >4 passengers or lack of alternatives.

7) Templates and canonical artifacts
- Access Request Template: templates/access-request-2027.md
- RFC Template (Change Management): templates/change-rfc-2027.md
- Vendor Intake Form: templates/vendor-intake-2027.pdf
- Procurement PO Template: templates/po-template-2027.xlsx
- Expense Report Template: templates/expense-report-2027.csv

Example RFC snippet (minimal valid front matter)
```
Title: "Schema migration for user_profiles"
Author: "Samir Patel"
Date: "2028-01-08"
Class: "Major"
Impact: ["auth-service", "billing-service"]
Rollout: "Canary 5% -> 25% -> 100% over 72 hours"
RollbackCriteria: "Error rate > 1% sustained for 5 minutes or >20% increase in tail latency"
```

8) Governance, metrics, and reporting
- Monthly operations review: owners report on outstanding access exceptions, RFC backlog, vendor renewals, and procurement spend vs. forecast.
- KPIs to track: average access request approval time, RFC cycle time, number of vendor incidents, expense report turnaround time.

Change log (recent updates)
- 2027-11-02: Initial baseline document (created).
- 2028-01-15: Added detailed vendor onboarding checklist and scoring rubric.
- 2028-02-14: Updated access approval SLA and added emergency Break Glass procedure.

How to use this page
- Use the templates link above to raise requests and RFCs. For area-specific nuances (e.g., RBAC for Dedicated clusters, private deployment KMS requirements) follow the linked pages in Related Pages.

Questions or exceptions
- Submit exceptions to governance@redwood.ai with business justification; exceptions are time-boxed and require a sponsor and compensating controls.

Acknowledgements
- Contributors: Diego Morales (Eng Platform), Priya Nair (Security), Samir Patel (Finance), Maya Lopez (People Ops).

Appendix: Contact matrix
- Procurement: procurement@redwood.ai
- Finance: finance@redwood.ai
- Security: rbac@redwood.ai
- Privacy & Legal: privacy@redwood.ai
- People Ops: people-ops@redwood.ai

source.title: Authorization and Travel Matrix + Operational Templates
source.author: Maya Chen
source.content:
Overview:
This playbook consolidates authorization patterns, approval matrices, change-management steps, data handling rules, procurement and vendor onboarding flow, and travel approval templates into a single operational reference. It is intended for cross-functional use by hiring managers, finance, IT/security, engineering managers, and program owners.

Goals:
- Provide a single source of truth for who can approve which requests and at what thresholds.
- Reduce approval friction by standardizing templates and SLA expectations.
- Improve audit readiness through explicit ownership and retention rules.

1) Access & Permissions Matrix
Summary:
Provide role-based access patterns for common systems (G-Suite, AWS, Redwood Console, Dedicated Admin, Private Deployments). Use least privilege and just-in-time access where feasible.

Matrix (summary table):
- Role: Employee; Systems: G-Suite (basic), Redwood Console (read); Default approver: Manager; Approval SLA: 2 business days.
- Role: Developer; Systems: Internal Git repos, Redwood staging; Default approver: Team Lead; Approval SLA: 2 business days.
- Role: Production Operator; Systems: Redwood Dedicated, Prod infra; Default approver: SRE Manager + Security Reviewer; Approval SLA: 1 business day.
- Role: Vendor Contractor; Systems: Scoped VPN, Redwood Console (limited); Default approver: Contract Owner + Security; Approval SLA: 3 business days.

Request patterns and required artifacts:
- New access request: justification, expiry date (30/90/180 days), data classification label, risk level.
- Emergency elevated access: pager/incident link, time-boxed (max 8 hours), post-incident review required.

2) Change Management (applies to infra, runtime, API contract changes)
Principles:
- Small, reversible changes are preferred.
- Critical-path changes require canary rollouts and automatic rollback hooks.

Change types and approval flow:
- Standard (non-production code/config change): submit change ticket, peer review, CI green => deploy to staging. Owner: author.
- Major (schema/API breaking, infra scaling changes): design review doc + ADR required, signoffs: owning product manager, infra lead, security, and at least one reliability reviewer. Schedule change window and define rollback.
- Emergency (P0/P1 incidents): follow incident runbook. Post-mortem required within 7 days with remediation plan.

Change SLA & gate checks:
- All Major changes must have an ADR published >= 72 hours before scheduled rollout.
- Canary duration: minimum 30 minutes or 10k requests, whichever is longer.

Checklist (pre-deploy):
1. Testing matrix completed (unit, integration, perf).
2. Monitoring dashboards updated.
3. Fallback path defined.
4. Rollback playbook attached.

3) Data Management and Handling Rules
Classification labels: public | internal | confidential | regulated.
Rules by label:
- Public: no restrictions.
- Internal: storage in internal buckets only; default encryption; retention default 2 years.
- Confidential: restrict to VPC-only storage; access via approval matrix; retention per data owner.
- Regulated: follow region-specific retention, encryption with customer-managed keys (KMS/HSM), audit logging for all access.

Required documentation on data usage:
- Data processing description (purpose + retention).
- Data flow diagram (plain-text or ascii) showing ingress/egress.
- Owners and contact info.

4) Procurement, Expenses & Vendor Management
Procurement thresholds and approval matrix:
- Low-touch purchases: <= $2,000, team budget owner approval.
- Mid-tier: $2,001 - $50,000, require Finance manager + approving director.
- Large / Capital expenditures: > $50,000, require Finance Director + Legal + Exec sponsor.

Standard vendor onboarding steps (checklist):
1. Vendor risk assessment (security questionnaire).
2. Contract review by Legal.
3. Finance set-up and PO issuance.
4. Access scoping (least privilege) and expiry.
5. Quarterly vendor reviews for critical vendors.

Expense rules:
- Per-diem caps and country-specific rates are in the Travel section.
- Receipts required for all expenses >= $25.
- Personal charges on company cards must be reimbursed within 14 days.

5) Travel Policy (operational template + matrix)
Booking and approval flow:
- All travel: submit travel request in Travel Portal with itinerary, cost estimate, and business justification.
- Approvers: Manager approves travel; for international travel or expense > $5,000 require Finance review.
- Travel Safety: Security must review travel to high-risk regions (>moderate advisory) before approvals.

Per-diem and allowable expenses (examples):
- Meals: up to $75/day domestic, $120/day international (region-adjusted).
- Lodging: reasonable mid-market hotel; use corporate negotiated rates where available.

Emergency and Duty of Care:
- Employees must register trips via Travel Portal. Security team monitors travel advisories. In case of evacuation, contact Security Ops immediately (security-ops@redwood.com).

6) Templates and Sample Artifacts
Included templates (copy/paste-ready):
- Access Request form (fields: requester, system, role, justification, expiry, manager).
- Change Request checklist (fields: change type, risk, rollforward/rollback commands, test plan).
- Vendor Onboarding intake form (vendor name, contact, data access required, security posture summary).
- Travel Request template (itinerary, objectives, estimated cost, manager approval).

Sample Access Request (plain):
- Requester: Jane Doe
- System: Redwood Dedicated - prod-metrics
- Role: Read-only
- Justification: Support quarterly latency study
- Expiry: 90 days
- Approver: Team Lead (signoff)

7) Ownership, Audit, and Review Cadence
- Policy owner: Finance-Legal (primary), Security (co-owner for access/data policies).
- Review cadence: full policy review annually; access matrix audited quarterly.
- Audit artifacts retention: access approvals and vendor agreements stored 7 years.

8) Incident and Escalation Paths
- Unauthorized access detected: notify Security Pager, revoke affected credentials, conduct time-boxed investigation (48 hours).
- Failed change causing outage: escalate to Oncall SRE, initiate rollback; postmortem published to eng-oncall within 72 hours.

9) Quick Reference: Approvals and SLAs
- Standard access requests: 2 business days.
- Production operator access: 1 business day + security review.
- Vendor onboarding baseline: 10 business days (assuming questionnaire returned).

10) Appendix: CLI snippets and policy links
- Example: create temporary access ticket (internal CLI):
  rw-ops access request --system prod --role read-only --expires 90d --justification "latency study"

Related pages and references:
- See: /confluence/eng-sre/runbooks/incident-process/ (incident runbook)
- See: /confluence/security-and-compliance/rbac-and-auth/ (RBAC standards)

Contact and escalation:
- Policy owner: Maya Chen (maya@redwood.com)
- Security co-owner: Priya Natarajan (priya@redwood.com)
- Finance contact: Alejandro Ruiz (alejandro@redwood.com)

Change log:
- 2026-01-15: Document created.
- 2026-06-20: Added emergency access pattern and JIT guidance.
- 2026-10-03: Updated travel per-diem and vendor onboarding SLA.

source.title: Policy & Process Almanac: Company-wide Standards and Templates
source.author: Priya Menon
source.body:
Overview:

This document is the single-source reference for company-wide policies and process templates across Access & Permissions, Change Management, Data Management, Procurement & Expenses, Vendor Management, Travel, and Internal Templates. Intended audience: people-ops, finance, engineering, product, security, and managers who must follow or approve cross-functional requests.

Goals:
- Provide clear, actionable procedures for common requests and approvals.
- Reduce turnaround time by standardizing thresholds, owners, and templates.
- Ensure security and compliance controls are applied consistently.

Scope and applicability:
- Applies to all Redwood Inference employees, contractors, and people with accounts on Redwood-managed systems.
- Exceptions require documented approval via the Exceptions process (see Escalation & Exceptions).

Quick policy matrix (summary):
| Domain | Primary owner | Typical SLA / Threshold | Key artifacts |
|---|---:|---|---|
| Access & Permissions | people-ops / security | Standard requests: 3 business days; Emergency elevated access: 2 hours | Access request template, RBAC group list |
| Change Management | eng-platform | Normal changes: 5 business days pre-notice; Major infra changes require CAB approval | Change request template, rollback playbook |
| Data Management | security / finance-legal | Data deletion requests: 10 business days; Retention: defined per classification | Data classification matrix, retention schedule |
| Procurement & Expenses | finance-legal | Purchases <$5k: 3 business days; >$50k: exec approval + 20 business days | PO template, vendor evaluation checklist |
| Vendor Management | finance-legal / security | New vendor onboarding: 15 business days | Vendor questionnaire, contract template |
| Travel | people-ops / finance-legal | Pre-approval: 5 business days; Expense submission: within 15 days of return | Travel request & expense templates |

1) Access & Permissions
Principles:
- Principle of least privilege by default.
- Temporary elevation preferred over long-lived high privileges.
- All privileged access requires an owner and an audit TTL.

Role definitions (examples):
- Reader: read-only to resources; auto-approver via manager.
- Contributor: ability to create resources; manager + tech lead approval.
- Admin: elevated privileges; security review + VP approval for permanent access.

Standard request flow (self-service for most):
1. Employee opens Access Request using the access_request template (link in Templates section).
2. Manager verifies business justification and approves in the system.
3. Technical owner approves (for contributor/admin).
4. Provisioning occurs via IaC or identity provider (IdP) automation.
5. Access is auto-expired after TTL unless renewed via a documented review.

Access thresholds requiring extra review:
- Any request granting "Admin" or "Privileged" role on production systems requires security review.
- Cross-tenant/VPC access requires network security sign-off and on-call notification.

Example CLI request (for advanced teams using the RBAC API):
```
curl -X POST https://identity.redwood.example/api/v1/access-requests \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"user":"alice@redwood.com","role":"prod-db-contributor","expiry":"2026-04-15","justification":"incident debug"}'
```
RBAC groups (current canonical list):
- redwood-readonly
- redwood-devs
- redwood-prod-ops
- redwood-admins
(Updates to the canonical list must be proposed via the Change Management process and documented in the RBAC registry.)

2) Change Management
Change types and required approvals:
- Minor (non-production config changes, docs): owner approval.
- Standard (code deploy, infra config): owner + release manager approval.
- Major (schema migrations, network changes, infra rollouts): CAB approval (weekly CAB meeting).
- Emergency (P1/P0 incidents): follow the Incident Commander's emergency change flow; retrospective required.

Change lifecycle checklist (before scheduling):
- Define impact window and rollback plan.
- Confirm pre-deployment validation tests.
- Notify stakeholders via the release calendar (email + Slack channel).
- Schedule change outside blackout windows unless emergency.

Pre-approval windows and SLAs:
- Standard changes: submit 5 business days before planned date.
- Major changes: submit 10 business days before planned date.
- The CAB meets every Tuesday at 09:00 PT; decisions recorded in the change tracker.

Rollback policy:
- Every change must include a tested rollback procedure that can be completed within the rollback window (target: 60 minutes for infra rollbacks).
- If rollback cannot be completed automatically, include manual runbook steps with required on-call contacts.

Change request template (brief):
- Title, owner, scope, start/end window, rollback steps, validation plan, stakeholder list, risk level.

3) Data Management
Classification levels:
- PUBLIC: safe to share externally. Retention: 3 years unless specified.
- INTERNAL: company-internal; retention as required by business.
- CONFIDENTIAL: PII, financials, customer data; retention per legal & contract.
- RESTRICTED: regulated data (HIPAA, PCI, etc.) — special handling and encryption at rest + in transit.

Retention & deletion:
- See the Data Retention Schedule table below for defaults. Deletions must be recorded in the Data Deletion Log.

Data retention schedule (examples):
| Data class | Default retention | Owner |
|---|---:|---|
| Public assets (marketing) | 3 years | Marketing lead |
| Internal analytics | 2 years | Data Platform team |
| Customer logs (non-PII) | 1 year | Customer Success |
| Customer PII | as contracted; minimum 7 years | Legal 

Encryption & key management:
- All CONFIDENTIAL and RESTRICTED data must be encrypted with customer-managed keys where contractually required.
- KMS usage: AWS KMS + HSM integration for private deployments; key rotation every 365 days recommended.

Data transfer checklist (example YAML template for cross-region movement):
```
transfer_request:
  owner: eng-data-platform
  source: us-west-2-pipeline
  destination: eu-central-1-lake
  data_class: INTERNAL
  approval: [security, legal]
  date_required: 2026-04-01
```

4) Procurement & Expenses
Procurement tiers & approval matrix:
| Tier | Value | Approver(s) |
|---|---:|---|
| Tier 1 | <$5,000 | Requester manager |
| Tier 2 | $5,000 - $50,000 | Manager + Finance (budget owner) |
| Tier 3 | >$50,000 | Director + Finance + Legal review (if third party) |

Purchase order (PO) process:
1. Raise a PO request in the procurement tool with supplier info and SOW.
2. Finance verifies budget and issues PO.
3. Contracts team reviews if third-party software/service.
4. Supplier receipt must match PO; invoices routed to AP for three-way match.

Expense policy (travel and non-travel):
- Employees must submit expenses within 15 calendar days of transaction.
- Meals and incidental per diem capped per location (see Travel section).
- Unapproved or late submissions may be delayed or denied.

Allowed payment methods:
- Company card: primary for travel and approved subscriptions.
- Personal reimbursements: supported with receipts and manager approval.

5) Vendor Management
Onboarding checklist (new vendor):
1. Vendor Profile: legal name, tax ID, region.
2. Security Questionnaire: SOC 2/ISO attestation or complete CAIQ.
3. Contract review: legal and procurement.
4. Risk assessment: data access, SSO, encryption requirements.
5. PO / payment terms setup.

Vendor risk levels and controls:
- Low: consultants without access to customer data — standard contract.
- Medium: vendors with limited system access — SSO + scoped permissions + annual review.
- High: vendors with customer data or production access — security controls, quarterly audits, and encryption requirements in contract.

Renewal & offboarding:
- Notify stakeholders 60 days before contract end for renewals.
- Offboarding checklist includes termination of all credentials, return/destruction of data, and deletion from asset registry.

6) Travel Policies
Booking & approvals:
- Use the corporate travel tool for all business travel.
- Domestic trips <48 hours require manager pre-approval. International travel requires security & people-ops notification.

Per diem & reimbursements:
- Per diem rates follow the government locality table adopted by Redwood; see travel-per-diem template.
- Economy class for flights under 6 hours; premium economy or business requires director approval.

Expense submission steps:
1. Submit expense within 15 days of return with itemized receipts.
2. Manager approves in expense system.
3. Finance processes payment on the next payroll cycle.

Safety & emergency contact:
- Before international travel, register trip with people-ops and provide emergency contact information.

7) Internal Templates & Quick Links
Included templates (copy/paste into the request tools):
- Access request template: (fields) requester, manager, role, expiry, justification, technical owner.
- Change request template: (fields) title, owner, windows, rollback steps, validation steps.
- Vendor onboarding checklist (as described above).

Sample Access Request (one-line):
Title: Temporary PROD DB Contributor
Requester: alice@redwood.com
Manager: bob@redwood.com
Role: prod-db-contributor
Expiry: 2026-04-12
Justification: customer incident debug

8) Roles & Ownership (contact pointers)
- people-ops: people-ops@redwood.example (policy questions, travel, benefits)
- finance-legal: procure@redwood.example (POs, vendor contracts)
- security: sec-eng@redwood.example (access reviews, data policy)
- eng-platform: infra@redwood.example (change board, release calendar)

9) Escalations & Exceptions
Exception request process:
1. Submit an Exceptions Request document with justification and compensating controls.
2. Owner team reviews within 5 business days.
3. If risk > medium, route to the Cross-Functional Exceptions Panel (CFEP) for final decision.

Emergency escalation flow (P0/P1):
- Page on-call via PagerDuty -> Incident Commander -> Notify CTO and Head of Security -> Temporary emergency change allowed with post-incident CAB review.

Appendix: Useful links and artifacts
- Access request template: /confluence/templates/access-request
- Change request tracker: /confluence/tools/change-tracker
- Data retention schedule: /confluence/policies-and-process/data-retention-2025
- PO template: /confluence/templates/purchase-order
- Vendor questionnaire (CAIQ): /confluence/security-and-compliance/vendor-caiaq

Revision history:
- 2026-01-12: Created by Priya Menon; initial policy draft.

title: Policy & Process Almanac: Company-wide Standards and Templates
preview:
Overview:

This document is the single-source reference for company-wide policies and process templates across Access & Permissions, Change Management, Data Management, Procurement & Expenses, Vendor Management, Travel, and Internal Templates. Intended audience: people-ops, finance, engineering, product, security, and managers who must follow or approve cross-functional requests.

Goals:
- Provide clear, actionable procedures for common requests and approvals.
- Reduce turnaround time by standardizing thresholds, owners, and templates.
- Ensure security and compliance controls are applied consistently.

Scope and applicability:
- Applies to all Redwood Inference employees, contractors, and people with accounts on Redwood-managed systems.
- Exceptions require documented approval via the Exceptions process (see Escalation & Exceptions).

Quick policy matrix (summary):
| Domain | Primary owner | Typical SLA / Threshold | Key artifacts |
|---|---:|---|---|
| Access & Permissions | people-ops / security | Standard requests: 3 business days; Emergency elevated access: 2 hours | Access request template, RBAC group list |
| Change Management | eng-platform | Normal changes: 5 business days pre-notice; Major infra changes require CAB approval | Change request template, rollback playbook |
| Data Management | security / finance-legal | Data deletion requests: 10 business days; Retention: defined per classification | Data classification matrix, retention schedule |
| Procurement & Expenses | finance-legal | Purchases <$5k: 3 business days; >$50k: exec approval + 20 business days | PO template, vendor evaluation checklist |
| Vendor Management | finance-legal / security | New vendor onboarding: 15 business days | Vendor questionnaire, contract template |
| Travel | people-ops / finance-legal | Pre-approval: 5 business days; Expense submission: within 15 days of return | Travel request & expense templates |

1) Access & Permissions
Principles:
- Principle of least privilege by default.
- Temporary elevation preferred over long-lived high privileges.
- All privileged access requires an owner and an audit TTL.

Role definitions (examples):
- Reader: read-only to resources; auto-approver via manager.
- Contributor: ability to create resources; manager + tech lead approval.
- Admin: elevated privileges; security review + VP approval for permanent access.

Standard request flow (self-service for most):
1. Employee opens Access Request using the access_request template (link in Templates section).
2. Manager verifies business justification and approves in the system.
3. Technical owner approves (for contributor/admin).
4. Provisioning occurs via IaC or identity provider (IdP) automation.
5. Access is auto-expired after TTL unless renewed via a documented review.

Access thresholds requiring extra review:
- Any request granting \"Admin\" or \"Privileged\" role on production systems requires security review.
- Cross-tenant/VPC access requires network security sign-off and on-call notification.

Example CLI request (for advanced teams using the RBAC API):
```
curl -X POST https://identity.redwood.example/api/v1/access-requests \\
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN' \\
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\
  -d '{"user":"alice@redwood.com","role":"prod-db-contributor","expiry":"2026-04-15","justification":"incident debug"}'
```
RBAC groups (current canonical list):
- redwood-readonly
- redwood-devs
- redwood-prod-ops
- redwood-admins
(Updates to the canonical list must be proposed via the Change Management process and documented in the RBAC registry.)

2) Change Management
Change types and required approvals:
- Minor (non-production config changes, docs): owner approval.
- Standard (code deploy, infra config): owner + release manager approval.
- Major (schema migrations, network changes, infra rollouts): CAB approval (weekly CAB meeting).
- Emergency (P1/P0 incidents): follow the Incident Commander's emergency change flow; retrospective required.

Change lifecycle checklist (before scheduling):
- Define impact window and rollback plan.
- Confirm pre-deployment validation tests.
- Notify stakeholders via the release calendar (email + Slack channel).
- Schedule change outside blackout windows unless emergency.

Pre-approval windows and SLAs:
- Standard changes: submit 5 business days before planned date.
- Major changes: submit 10 business days before planned date.
- The CAB meets every Tuesday at 09:00 PT; decisions recorded in the change tracker.

Rollback policy:
- Every change must include a tested rollback procedure that can be completed within the rollback window (target: 60 minutes for infra rollbacks).
- If rollback cannot be completed automatically, include manual runbook steps with required on-call contacts.

Change request template (brief):
- Title, owner, scope, start/end window, rollback steps, validation plan, stakeholder list, risk level.

3) Data Management
Classification levels:
- PUBLIC: safe to share externally. Retention: 3 years unless specified.
- INTERNAL: company-internal; retention as required by business.
- CONFIDENTIAL: PII, financials, customer data; retention per legal & contract.
- RESTRICTED: regulated data (HIPAA, PCI, etc.) — special handling and encryption at rest + in transit.

Retention & deletion:
- See the Data Retention Schedule table below for defaults. Deletions must be recorded in the Data Deletion Log.

Data retention schedule (examples):
| Data class | Default retention | Owner |
|---|---:|---|
| Public assets (marketing) | 3 years | Marketing lead |
| Internal analytics | 2 years | Data Platform team |
| Customer logs (non-PII) | 1 year | Customer Success |
| Customer PII | as contracted; minimum 7 years | Legal 

Encryption & key management:
- All CONFIDENTIAL and RESTRICTED data must be encrypted with customer-managed keys where contractually required.
- KMS usage: AWS KMS + HSM integration for private deployments; key rotation every 365 days recommended.

Data transfer checklist (example YAML template for cross-region movement):
```
transfer_request:
  owner: eng-data-platform
  source: us-west-2-pipeline
  destination: eu-central-1-lake
  data_class: INTERNAL
  approval: [security, legal]
  date_required: 2026-04-01
```

4) Procurement & Expenses
Procurement tiers & approval matrix:
| Tier | Value | Approver(s) |
|---|---:|---|
| Tier 1 | <$5,000 | Requester manager |
| Tier 2 | $5,000 - $50,000 | Manager + Finance (budget owner) |
| Tier 3 | >$50,000 | Director + Finance + Legal review (if third party) |

Purchase order (PO) process:
1. Raise a PO request in the procurement tool with supplier info and SOW.
2. Finance verifies budget and issues PO.
3. Contracts team reviews if third-party software/service.
4. Supplier receipt must match PO; invoices routed to AP for three-way match.

Expense policy (travel and non-travel):
- Employees must submit expenses within 15 calendar days of transaction.
- Meals and incidental per diem capped per location (see Travel section).
- Unapproved or late submissions may be delayed or denied.

Allowed payment methods:
- Company card: primary for travel and approved subscriptions.
- Personal reimbursements: supported with receipts and manager approval.

5) Vendor Management
Onboarding checklist (new vendor):
1. Vendor Profile: legal name, tax ID, region.
2. Security Questionnaire: SOC 2/ISO attestation or complete CAIQ.
3. Contract review: legal and procurement.
4. Risk assessment: data access, SSO, encryption requirements.
5. PO / payment terms setup.

Vendor risk levels and controls:
- Low: consultants without access to customer data — standard contract.
- Medium: vendors with limited system access — SSO + scoped permissions + annual review.
- High: vendors with customer data or production access — security controls, quarterly audits, and encryption requirements in contract.

Renewal & offboarding:
- Notify stakeholders 60 days before contract end for renewals.
- Offboarding checklist includes termination of all credentials, return/destruction of data, and deletion from asset registry.

6) Travel Policies
Booking & approvals:
- Use the corporate travel tool for all business travel.
- Domestic trips <48 hours require manager pre-approval. International travel requires security & people-ops notification.

Per diem & reimbursements:
- Per diem rates follow the government locality table adopted by Redwood; see travel-per-diem template.
- Economy class for flights under 6 hours; premium economy or business requires director approval.

Expense submission steps:
1. Submit expense within 15 days of return with itemized receipts.
2. Manager approves in expense system.
3. Finance processes payment on the next payroll cycle.

Safety & emergency contact:
- Before international travel, register trip with people-ops and provide emergency contact information.

7) Internal Templates & Quick Links
Included templates (copy/paste into the request tools):
- Access request template: (fields) requester, manager, role, expiry, justification, technical owner.
- Change request template: (fields) title, owner, windows, rollback steps, validation steps.
- Vendor onboarding checklist (as described above).

Sample Access Request (one-line):
Title: Temporary PROD DB Contributor
Requester: alice@redwood.com
Manager: bob@redwood.com
Role: prod-db-contributor
Expiry: 2026-04-12
Justification: customer incident debug

8) Roles & Ownership (contact pointers)
- people-ops: people-ops@redwood.example (policy questions, travel, benefits)
- finance-legal: procure@redwood.example (POs, vendor contracts)
- security: sec-eng@redwood.example (access reviews, data policy)
- eng-platform: infra@redwood.example (change board, release calendar)

9) Escalations & Exceptions
Exception request process:
1. Submit an Exceptions Request document with justification and compensating controls.
2. Owner team reviews within 5 business days.
3. If risk > medium, route to the Cross-Functional Exceptions Panel (CFEP) for final decision.

Emergency escalation flow (P0/P1):
- Page on-call via PagerDuty -> Incident Commander -> Notify CTO and Head of Security -> Temporary emergency change allowed with post-incident CAB review.

Appendix: Useful links and artifacts
- Access request template: /confluence/templates/access-request
- Change request tracker: /confluence/tools/change-tracker
- Data retention schedule: /confluence/policies-and-process/data-retention-2025
- PO template: /confluence/templates/purchase-order
- Vendor questionnaire (CAIQ): /confluence/security-and-compliance/vendor-caiaq

Revision history:
- 2026-01-12: Created by Priya Menon; initial policy draft.
- 2026-02-20: Added procurement tiers and vendor renewal timings (reviewers Marco Alvarez, Sofia Patel).
- 2026-03-05: Finalized RBAC group canonical list and CLI example; published.

End of document.

source.title: Metering & Billing Data Retention and Access Controls Policy
source.author: Mei Lin
source.body:
## Purpose
This policy defines Redwood Inference’s requirements for **retention**, **access control**, and **audit logging** for metering and billing datasets used for:
- Customer invoicing and credits
- Revenue recognition support (RevRec) and audit readiness
- Pricing/packaging analytics and experimentation
- Support dispute investigation and customer reporting
- Engineering reliability (meter sanity, drift detection) and incident response

The intent is to maintain **high-integrity, auditable billing telemetry** while minimizing sensitive data exposure and ensuring separation of duties.

## Scope
This policy applies to:
- **Hosted API** metering (tokens/requests/embeddings/rerank)
- **Dedicated** usage metering (GPU-seconds and pool-level allocation)
- Data flows from request logs → usage events → billing ledger → customer-facing usage exports
- All environments that store or process metering/billing data (prod, staging where prod data is copied)

**Out of scope:** general application analytics unrelated to billing meters (see Analytics Data Policy), and raw model outputs stored by customers.

## Guiding principles
1. **Least privilege by default.** Access is granted only to the minimum datasets/columns needed.
2. **Purpose-based access.** Access approvals must state an allowed purpose (see “Allowed purposes”).
3. **Separation of duties.** Where possible, the people who implement meter logic are not the only approvers for financial “source of truth” datasets.
4. **Minimize sensitive payloads.** Metering/billing datasets must not retain prompt/completion text unless explicitly approved as an exception.
5. **Auditable trail.** All access to restricted datasets is logged and reviewable.
6. **Time-bounded grants.** Elevated access expires and requires re-approval.

## Definitions
- **Metering data:** event-level or aggregated records describing usage (e.g., prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, billed_tokens, requests, cache_hit flags, model, region).
- **Billing ledger (source of truth):** the authoritative dataset used to generate invoices and support RevRec.
- **Restricted dataset:** customer-sensitive data (billing amounts, credits, contract terms, customer identifiers beyond what’s needed) requiring explicit access controls and audit logging.
- **Customer-sensitive data:** any data that could materially impact a customer invoice, reveal pricing/discounts, or contain regulated identifiers.

## Data classification for metering & billing
All metering/billing datasets are classified as **restricted** unless explicitly designated “internal” by Finance/Legal.

### Content minimization requirements
Metering/billing tables and exports MUST NOT include:
- Prompt text, completion text, tool arguments, file contents, or user-provided documents
- End-user identifiers in plaintext (email, phone, government IDs)

Metering/billing tables MAY include:
- Customer account identifiers (internal account_id / org_id)
- API key identifiers (hashed or surrogate ids; no raw secret material)
- Request identifiers (request_id, idempotency_key) needed for dedupe and dispute resolution
- Usage dimensions (timestamp, model, region, endpoint, cache_hit, status class)

If a debugging workflow requires content inspection, it must use a **separate, tightly controlled debug pipeline** with Security approval and short retention (see “Exceptions”).

## Systems and canonical datasets (summary)
The following names reflect the common internal naming used in Snowflake/warehouse conventions. Exact schemas may differ by environment.

**Event & aggregation layer**
- `telemetry.usage_events_v1` / `telemetry.usage_events_v2`
- `telemetry.request_rollups_hourly` (aggregations)

**Billing source of truth**
- `billing.billing_ledger` (invoice line item inputs)
- `billing.credits_ledger` (credits/grants application)
- `billing.invoice_preview_runs` (pre-send validation outputs)

**Reconciliation exports (Finance-facing)**
- `finance_billing.recon_export_daily` (stable, documented schema)
- `finance_billing.meter_drift_checks` (drift metrics by account/model/region)

**Customer-facing**
- Usage API export tables feeding the customer usage endpoints (e.g., `customer_usage.daily_usage_exports`)

## Retention policy
Retention balances audit requirements (RevRec, statutory retention) with minimization of sensitive data.

### Retention schedule
| Data type | Examples / tables | Default retention | Storage notes | Owner |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Raw request logs (non-content) | request metadata logs without prompt/completion text | 30 days queryable + 60 days cold | Cold storage must remain restricted; no prompt/completion payloads | Engineering (Telemetry) |
| Usage events (event-level metering) | `telemetry.usage_events_*` | 25 months queryable | Designed to cover current + prior fiscal year analyses and dispute windows | Engineering (Telemetry) / Finance |
| Aggregated usage rollups | hourly/daily rollups used by dashboards | 36 months queryable | Aggregations must be derivable from event data; store for performance | Engineering (Observability) |
| Billing ledger (source of truth) | `billing.billing_ledger`, invoice inputs | 7 years | Archived after 25 months to low-cost immutable storage with retrieval process | Finance (RevRec) |
| Credits and adjustments | `billing.credits_ledger`, adjustments | 7 years | Includes approvals and references to ticket IDs | Finance (RevRec) |
| Invoice previews and close artifacts | `billing.invoice_preview_runs`, close check outputs | 7 years | Treated as audit evidence | Finance (RevRec) |
| Audit logs (data access) | Snowflake access history, export logs | 12 months queryable + 24 months archived | Must be tamper-evident; access restricted to Security + Finance | Security & Compliance |
| Customer-facing usage exports | daily/monthly export artifacts | 25 months | Aligned to usage event queryable period | Product/Engineering |

### Deletion and archival requirements
- **Automated TTL enforcement** is required for tables with finite retention (e.g., request logs, usage events queryable tier).
- When data moves from queryable to archived tiers:
  - Archived objects must be **immutable** (write-once where supported) and access-controlled.
  - Retrieval must be via a documented request process (ticket + owner approval).
- Any retention changes must be treated as a policy change (see “Change management”).

## Access control policy
### Allowed purposes
Access to restricted metering/billing datasets may be approved only for the following purposes:
1. **Billing operations:** invoice generation, previews, credit application, invoice corrections with approved process.
2. **RevRec & audit support:** revenue recognition tie-outs, audit evidence, close checklists, materiality analysis.
3. **Customer support disputes:** investigating a customer-reported discrepancy, validating exports, preparing customer response.
4. **Engineering reliability:** meter drift detection, incident response, data-quality SLO monitoring.
5. **Pricing/packaging analytics:** experiments, margin model inputs, cohort analysis (prefer aggregated/anonymized views).
6. **Legal/commercial:** contract definition validation, dispute handling, litigation hold (when invoked).

Any other purpose requires explicit approval from **CFO (or delegate)** and **GC (or delegate)**.

### Access model (implementation)
- **Primary enforcement:** Snowflake RBAC roles + schema-level and column-level access controls.
- **Identity source:** Okta group membership synced to Snowflake roles.
- **Service accounts:** must use separate named roles; no shared credentials.
- **Production vs staging:** production restricted datasets must not be copied into staging unless approved, redacted, and time-bounded.

### Role-based access matrix (high level)
This matrix describes the intended access posture. Exact grants are managed in Snowflake role definitions.

| Function / team | Typical role | Access level | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Finance (RevRec/Accounting) | `ROLE_FINANCE_REVREC` | Read/write to billing ledger inputs, credits ledger; read to usage recon exports | Primary operational owner of “source of truth” tables |
| FP&A | `ROLE_FINANCE_FPA` | Read to aggregated usage and margin views; limited read to recon exports | No write access to billing ledger |
| Legal (GC/Commercial) | `ROLE_LEGAL_BILLING_VIEW` | Read to invoice artifacts and contract-linked billing summaries | No access to raw event-level data by default |
| Product Ops / Analytics | `ROLE_PRODUCTOPS_METERING` | Read to aggregated, de-identified views; limited read to usage exports | Event-level access requires explicit justification |
| RevOps (GTM analytics) | `ROLE_REVOPS_PRICING` | Read to packaging/plan attribution and aggregated spend | No access to credits/discount line-item details by default |
| Engineering (Telemetry owners) | `ROLE_ENG_TELEMETRY` | Read to usage events; write to telemetry pipeline outputs | No direct write access to billing ledger source-of-truth tables |
| SRE / On-call | `ROLE_ENG_SRE_METERING` | Read to drift dashboards, checks, and relevant aggregates | Event-level access is time-bounded during incidents |
| Support (Billing specialists) | `ROLE_SUPPORT_BILLING` | Read to customer usage exports + invoice summary views | Must not access other customers outside assigned case |
| Security & Compliance | `ROLE_SECURITY_AUDIT` | Read to access logs and audit evidence | Can approve/deny elevated access requests |

### Column-level restrictions (examples)
The following fields are treated as **high-sensitivity** and should be protected with column masking policies or separate secured views:
- Invoice amounts, negotiated rates, discount percentages, credit balances
- Contract identifiers and special terms flags
- Customer PII (if present in exceptional cases)

Where feasible, provide **approved views**:
- `*_public_metrics_view` (no customer identifiers)
- `*_account_rollup_view` (account_id present; no pricing fields)
- `*_finance_view` (includes pricing/credits; Finance only)

## Requesting access
### Standard access request process
1. File an internal access ticket (Data Access queue) with:
   - Requested role(s)
   - Purpose (must match “Allowed purposes”)
   - Duration needed (default 30 days)
   - Datasets required (tables/views)
2. Manager approval for the requester.
3. Data owner approval:
   - Finance owner for billing ledger and credit datasets
   - Telemetry owner for usage event datasets
4. Security review and grant:
   - Ensure least privilege; propose view-based access if possible
   - Configure expiration and logging requirements
5. Quarterly re-certification for persistent access roles.

### Emergency access (“break-glass”)
- Used only for active billing-impacting incidents or close-blockers.
- Requires on-call incident channel + ticket reference.
- Grants expire within 24 hours and require a follow-up review.

## Audit logging requirements
### What must be logged
For all restricted datasets:
- Role used (Snowflake role)
- User/service identity
- Time of access
- Objects accessed (database/schema/table/view)
- Query identifier and execution metadata
- Rows/bytes exported (where available)
- Export destination for approved exports

### Logging controls
- Access must occur through named roles (no personal default roles for restricted schemas).
- Prohibit or alert on:
  - Large result exports from restricted schemas
  - Bulk unloads to external stages without explicit approval
  - Queries that join restricted billing data with unrelated datasets

### Log retention and review cadence
- Queryable access logs retained for **12 months** minimum.
- Archived access logs retained for **24 months** minimum.
- Security reviews access anomalies **monthly**; Finance reviews Finance-role access **quarterly** (certification).

## Data exports and handling
- Prefer **server-side exports** to approved storage with encryption-at-rest and access logging.
- Avoid local downloads. If unavoidable (e.g., auditor request), the export must:
  - Be encrypted
  - Be time-bounded
  - Be stored only in approved secure locations
  - Be referenced in a ticket with approver name and expiry
- Customer-provided dispute packets (CSV exports, screenshots) must be stored in the customer support system and linked to the case; do not paste sensitive billing data into Slack.

## Month-end close controls (billing data freeze)
To support auditability and consistent RevRec:
- Finance defines a **usage data freeze window** for month-end close (published in the close checklist).
- Late-arriving events/backfills after the freeze:
  - Must not silently alter prior-period billed amounts without Finance/Legal approval
  - Must be recorded as an adjustment with an audit trail (ticket + approver)
- Meter changes overlapping experiment windows must be explicitly segmented in analysis outputs (pre/post meter fix).

## Exceptions
Exceptions to this policy (including retaining prompts/completions, expanding access to pricing/discount fields, or extending retention beyond defaults) require:
- Written justification
- Data owner approval
- Security approval
- Finance (RevRec) approval if billing/RevRec impacted
- Legal approval if contract terms, dispute posture, or regulated data is involved

All exceptions must include an expiry date and be tracked in the policy exceptions register.

## Enforcement
Violations may result in:
- Immediate revocation of access
- Security incident review
- Required retraining
- Disciplinary action up to termination (per company policy)

## Change management
- Policy changes require review by Finance (RevRec), Security, and Legal.
- Metering schema changes that impact billing must follow the metering change control process (shadow billing compare period, sign-off, and audit evidence).
- The document owner must re-review this policy at least **annually** or after any major billing pipeline redesign.

## Owners
- **Policy owner (Finance/Legal):** Emily Stone (RevRec)
- **Telemetry owner:** Logan Wright
- **Security owner:** Security & Compliance on-call rotation (reviewed by Aisha Bello)

## Changelog
- 2026-02-22: Clarified retention schedule for usage events vs billing ledger; added explicit audit log retention and quarterly recertification.
- 2026-01-18: Added column-level restriction guidance for pricing/credits; clarified allowed purposes for Product Ops.
- 2025-11-12: Initial publication.

title: Metering & Billing Data Retention and Access Controls Policy
preview:
## Purpose
This policy defines Redwood Inference’s requirements for **retention**, **access control**, and **audit logging** for metering and billing datasets used for:
- Customer invoicing and credits
- Revenue recognition support (RevRec) and audit readiness
- Pricing/packaging analytics and experimentation
- Support dispute investigation and customer reporting
- Engineering reliability (meter sanity, drift detection) and incident response

The intent is to maintain **high-integrity, auditable billing telemetry** while minimizing sensitive data exposure and ensuring separation of duties.

## Scope
This policy applies to:
- **Hosted API** metering (tokens/requests/embeddings/rerank)
- **Dedicated** usage metering (GPU-seconds and pool-level allocation)
- Data flows from request logs → usage events → billing ledger → customer-facing usage exports
- All environments that store or process metering/billing data (prod, staging where prod data is copied)

**Out of scope:** general application analytics unrelated to billing meters (see Analytics Data Policy), and raw model outputs stored by customers.

## Guiding principles
1. **Least privilege by default.** Access is granted only to the minimum datasets/columns needed.
2. **Purpose-based access.** Access approvals must state an allowed purpose (see “Allowed purposes”).
3. **Separation of duties.** Where possible, the people who implement meter logic are not the only approvers for financial “source of truth” datasets.
4. **Minimize sensitive payloads.** Metering/billing datasets must not retain prompt/completion text unless explicitly approved as an exception.
5. **Auditable trail.** All access to restricted datasets is logged and reviewable.
6. **Time-bounded grants.** Elevated access expires and requires re-approval.

## Definitions
- **Metering data:** event-level or aggregated records describing usage (e.g., prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, billed_tokens, requests, cache_hit flags, model, region).
- **Billing ledger (source of truth):** the authoritative dataset used to generate invoices and support RevRec.
- **Restricted dataset:** customer-sensitive data (billing amounts, credits, contract terms, customer identifiers beyond what’s needed) requiring explicit access controls and audit logging.
- **Customer-sensitive data:** any data that could materially impact a customer invoice, reveal pricing/discounts, or contain regulated identifiers.

## Data classification for metering & billing
All metering/billing datasets are classified as **restricted** unless explicitly designated “internal” by Finance/Legal.

### Content minimization requirements
Metering/billing tables and exports MUST NOT include:
- Prompt text, completion text, tool arguments, file contents, or user-provided documents
- End-user identifiers in plaintext (email, phone, government IDs)

Metering/billing tables MAY include:
- Customer account identifiers (internal account_id / org_id)
- API key identifiers (hashed or surrogate ids; no raw secret material)
- Request identifiers (request_id, idempotency_key) needed for dedupe and dispute resolution
- Usage dimensions (timestamp, model, region, endpoint, cache_hit, status class)

If a debugging workflow requires content inspection, it must use a **separate, tightly controlled debug pipeline** with Security approval and short retention (see “Exceptions”).

## Systems and canonical datasets (summary)
The following names reflect the common internal naming used in Snowflake/warehouse conventions. Exact schemas may differ by environment.

**Event & aggregation layer**
- `telemetry.usage_events_v1` / `telemetry.usage_events_v2`
- `telemetry.request_rollups_hourly` (aggregations)

**Billing source of truth**
- `billing.billing_ledger` (invoice line item inputs)
- `billing.credits_ledger` (credits/grants application)
- `billing.invoice_preview_runs` (pre-send validation outputs)

**Reconciliation exports (Finance-facing)**
- `finance_billing.recon_export_daily` (stable, documented schema)
- `finance_billing.meter_drift_checks` (drift metrics by account/model/region)

**Customer-facing**
- Usage API export tables feeding the customer usage endpoints (e.g., `customer_usage.daily_usage_exports`)

## Retention policy
Retention balances audit requirements (RevRec, statutory retention) with minimization of sensitive data.

### Retention schedule
| Data type | Examples / tables | Default retention | Storage notes | Owner |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Raw request logs (non-content) | request metadata logs without prompt/completion text | 30 days queryable + 60 days cold | Cold storage must remain restricted; no prompt/completion payloads | Engineering (Telemetry) |
| Usage events (event-level metering) | `telemetry.usage_events_*` | 25 months queryable | Designed to cover current + prior fiscal year analyses and dispute windows | Engineering (Telemetry) / Finance |
| Aggregated usage rollups | hourly/daily rollups used by dashboards | 36 months queryable | Aggregations must be derivable from event data; store for performance | Engineering (Observability) |
| Billing ledger (source of truth) | `billing.billing_ledger`, invoice inputs | 7 years | Archived after 25 months to low-cost immutable storage with retrieval process | Finance (RevRec) |
| Credits and adjustments | `billing.credits_ledger`, adjustments | 7 years | Includes approvals and references to ticket IDs | Finance (RevRec) |
| Invoice previews and close artifacts | `billing.invoice_preview_runs`, close check outputs | 7 years | Treated as audit evidence | Finance (RevRec) |
| Audit logs (data access) | Snowflake access history, export logs | 12 months queryable + 24 months archived | Must be tamper-evident; access restricted to Security + Finance | Security & Compliance |
| Customer-facing usage exports | daily/monthly export artifacts | 25 months | Aligned to usage event queryable period | Product/Engineering |

### Deletion and archival requirements
- **Automated TTL enforcement** is required for tables with finite retention (e.g., request logs, usage events queryable tier).
- When data moves from queryable to archived tiers:
  - Archived objects must be **immutable** (write-once where supported) and access-controlled.
  - Retrieval must be via a documented request process (ticket + owner approval).
- Any retention changes must be treated as a policy change (see “Change management”).

## Access control policy
### Allowed purposes
Access to restricted metering/billing datasets may be approved only for the following purposes:
1. **Billing operations:** invoice generation, previews, credit application, invoice corrections with approved process.
2. **RevRec & audit support:** revenue recognition tie-outs, audit evidence, close checklists, materiality analysis.
3. **Customer support disputes:** investigating a customer-reported discrepancy, validating exports, preparing customer response.
4. **Engineering reliability:** meter drift detection, incident response, data-quality SLO monitoring.
5. **Pricing/packaging analytics:** experiments, margin model inputs, cohort analysis (prefer aggregated/anonymized views).
6. **Legal/commercial:** contract definition validation, dispute handling, litigation hold (when invoked).

Any other purpose requires explicit approval from **CFO (or delegate)** and **GC (or delegate)**.

### Access model (implementation)
- **Primary enforcement:** Snowflake RBAC roles + schema-level and column-level access controls.
- **Identity source:** Okta group membership synced to Snowflake roles.
- **Service accounts:** must use separate named roles; no shared credentials.
- **Production vs staging:** production restricted datasets must not be copied into staging unless approved, redacted, and time-bounded.

### Role-based access matrix (high level)
This matrix describes the intended access posture. Exact grants are managed in Snowflake role definitions.

| Function / team | Typical role | Access level | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Finance (RevRec/Accounting) | `ROLE_FINANCE_REVREC` | Read/write to billing ledger inputs, credits ledger; read to usage recon exports | Primary operational owner of “source of truth” tables |
| FP&A | `ROLE_FINANCE_FPA` | Read to aggregated usage and margin views; limited read to recon exports | No write access to billing ledger |
| Legal (GC/Commercial) | `ROLE_LEGAL_BILLING_VIEW` | Read to invoice artifacts and contract-linked billing summaries | No access to raw event-level data by default |
| Product Ops / Analytics | `ROLE_PRODUCTOPS_METERING` | Read to aggregated, de-identified views; limited read to usage exports | Event-level access requires explicit justification |
| RevOps (GTM analytics) | `ROLE_REVOPS_PRICING` | Read to packaging/plan attribution and aggregated spend | No access to credits/discount line-item details by default |
| Engineering (Telemetry owners) | `ROLE_ENG_TELEMETRY` | Read to usage events; write to telemetry pipeline outputs | No direct write access to billing ledger source-of-truth tables |
| SRE / On-call | `ROLE_ENG_SRE_METERING` | Read to drift dashboards, checks, and relevant aggregates | Event-level access is time-bounded during incidents |
| Support (Billing specialists) | `ROLE_SUPPORT_BILLING` | Read to customer usage exports + invoice summary views | Must not access other customers outside assigned case |
| Security & Compliance | `ROLE_SECURITY_AUDIT` | Read to access logs and audit evidence | Can approve/deny elevated access requests |

### Column-level restrictions (examples)
The following fields are treated as **high-sensitivity** and should be protected with column masking policies or separate secured views:
- Invoice amounts, negotiated rates, discount percentages, credit balances
- Contract identifiers and special terms flags
- Customer PII (if present in exceptional cases)

Where feasible, provide **approved views**:
- `*_public_metrics_view` (no customer identifiers)
- `*_account_rollup_view` (account_id present; no pricing fields)
- `*_finance_view` (includes pricing/credits; Finance only)

## Requesting access
### Standard access request process
1. File an internal access ticket (Data Access queue) with:
   - Requested role(s)
   - Purpose (must match “Allowed purposes”)
   - Duration needed (default 30 days)
   - Datasets required (tables/views)
2. Manager approval for the requester.
3. Data owner approval:
   - Finance owner for billing ledger and credit datasets
   - Telemetry owner for usage event datasets
4. Security review and grant:
   - Ensure least privilege; propose view-based access if possible
   - Configure expiration and logging requirements
5. Quarterly re-certification for persistent access roles.

### Emergency access (“break-glass”)
- Used only for active billing-impacting incidents or close-blockers.
- Requires on-call incident channel + ticket reference.
- Grants expire within 24 hours and require a follow-up review.

## Audit logging requirements
### What must be logged
For all restricted datasets:
- Role used (Snowflake role)
- User/service identity
- Time of access
- Objects accessed (database/schema/table/view)
- Query identifier and execution metadata
- Rows/bytes exported (where available)
- Export destination for approved exports

### Logging controls
- Access must occur through named roles (no personal default roles for restricted schemas).
- Prohibit or alert on:
  - Large result exports from restricted schemas
  - Bulk unloads to external stages without explicit approval
  - Queries that join restricted billing data with unrelated datasets

### Log retention and review cadence
- Queryable access logs retained for **12 months** minimum.
- Archived access logs retained for **24 months** minimum.
- Security reviews access anomalies **monthly**; Finance reviews Finance-role access **quarterly** (certification).

## Data exports and handling
- Prefer **server-side exports** to approved storage with encryption-at-rest and access logging.
- Avoid local downloads. If unavoidable (e.g., auditor request), the export must:

  - Be stored only in approved secure locations
  - Be referenced in a ticket with approver name and expiry
- Customer-provided dispute packets (CSV exports, screenshots) must be stored in the customer support system and linked to the case; do not paste sensitive billing data into Slack.

## Month-end close controls (billing data freeze)
To support auditability and consistent RevRec:
- Finance defines a **usage data freeze window** for month-end close (published in the close checklist).
- Late-arriving events/backfills after the freeze:
  - Must not silently alter prior-period billed amounts without Finance/Legal approval
  - Must be recorded as an adjustment with an audit trail (ticket + approver)
- Meter changes overlapping experiment windows must be explicitly segmented in analysis outputs (pre/post meter fix).

## Exceptions
Exceptions to this policy (including retaining prompts/completions, expanding access to pricing/discount fields, or extending retention beyond defaults) require:
- Written justification
- Data owner approval
- Security approval
- Finance (RevRec) approval if billing/RevRec impacted
- Legal approval if contract terms, dispute posture, or regulated data is involved

All exceptions must include an expiry date and be tracked in the policy exceptions register.

## Enforcement
Violations may result in:
- Immediate revocation of access
- Security incident review
- Required retraining
- Disciplinary action up to termination (per company policy)

## Change management
- Policy changes require review by Finance (RevRec), Security, and Legal.
- Metering schema changes that impact billing must follow the metering change control process (shadow billing compare period, sign-off, and audit evidence).
- The document owner must re-review this policy at least **annually** or after any major billing pipeline redesign.

## Owners
- **Policy owner (Finance/Legal):** Emily Stone (RevRec)
- **Telemetry owner:** Logan Wright
- **Security owner:** Security & Compliance on-call rotation (reviewed by Aisha Bello)

## Changelog
